message:
chore: sync beads issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -459,6 +459,7 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","title":"[AA-10.3] Produce comparative demos, closure packet, and launch doctrine for runtime ascension","description":"Produce the final closure packet for the ascension program: comparative demos, claim-backed benchmark summaries, known-risk register, rollback doctrine, and the operator/developer guidance needed to continue or ship the work responsibly.","design":"Scope:\n- Summarize what actually improved, what remained experimental, and why.\n- Include side-by-side comparisons against conservative baselines and any valid external references.\n- Leave behind a package future contributors can extend without reconstructing the original intent.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Closure packet links every important claim to evidence, unit tests, workloads, structured logs, rollback triggers, and cross-track compatibility verdicts.
2. Comparative demos and deterministic E2E drill scripts distinguish default-ready work from experimental work and explain the user-visible difference.
3. Known risks, non-goals, downgrade paths, unsupported combinations, and next-step decisions are explicit.
4. Final docs, runnable verification commands, composition-drill recipes, structured incident logs, and operator/developer troubleshooting recipes are self-contained enough to act as the continuation and incident-response point for future sessions.","notes":"Primary risk: turning closure into marketing instead of truth.\nCountermeasure: every headline result must cite the underlying evidence graph.\nFallback: if a track lacks closure-quality evidence, it remains explicitly experimental in the launch doctrine.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldCanyon","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:15.209441938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T08:39:03.782770946Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","release","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:15.209441938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:05.571178582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:37.874942303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:08.062936829Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:10.619028203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:13.114106178Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6.1","title":"[AA-10.3][Prep] Scaffold runtime ascension closure packet + launch doctrine contract","description":"Create the truth-first contract scaffold for AA-10.3 closure work: a closure packet / launch doctrine doc, canonical artifact, smoke runner, and contract test. Scope is intentionally preparatory and must state current NO-GO / experimental surfaces explicitly until upstream closure blockers are resolved.","notes":"Completed; RusticRiver's prep scaffold is present in the working tree and the contract test now passes remotely.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:15:37.424946966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T07:37:18Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T07:37:18Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","release","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:15:37.424946966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2425,"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6.1","author":"CopperGlacier","text":"Normalized the bead closed after confirming RusticRiver's scaffold is present in `docs/runtime_ascension_closure_packet.md`, `artifacts/runtime_ascension_closure_packet_v1.json`, `scripts/run_runtime_ascension_closure_smoke.sh`, and `tests/runtime_ascension_closure_packet_contract.rs`. Fresh remote verification passed with `rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-copper-aa106 cargo test --test runtime_ascension_closure_packet_contract -- --nocapture` (22 tests).","created_at":"2026-03-07T07:37:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6.2","title":"[AA-10.3][Prep] Add evidence citation registry and rerun-command matrix to closure packet","description":"Tighten the AA-10.3 closure scaffold so every comparative demo and promoted surface in the runtime ascension closure packet cites explicit evidence artifacts and exact rerun commands in both the doc and canonical JSON artifact. This is preparatory work only: it must keep the packet in NO_GO mode where upstream blockers remain open, while making future closure refreshes more auditable and continuation-friendly.","notes":"Completed","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-07T06:38:42.482110937Z","created_by":"WildStone","updated_at":"2026-03-07T06:47:18.709834836Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T06:47:18.709712349Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","release","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:38:42.482110937Z","created_by":"WildStone","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6.3","title":"[AA-10.3][Runner] Make closure smoke runner repo-root deterministic and capture command provenance","description":"Harden scripts/run_runtime_ascension_closure_smoke.sh so smoke scenarios run from the repository root regardless of caller CWD, and record the exact executed commands/workdir in the emitted manifests/report. Align the closure packet doc/artifact/contract with the deterministic runner behavior.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MistyCrane","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-07T23:39:14.962764004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T23:39:14.962764004Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","release","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:39:14.962764004Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","title":"[AA-10.4] Run cross-track composition gauntlet, compatibility matrix, and incident drill pack","description":"Execute the full-system validation layer that the per-track beads do not cover on their own: deterministic composition tests across interacting advanced features, a compatibility matrix, and operator-grade incident drill artifacts.","design":"Scope:
- Compose the graduating tracks in the combinations that matter to real users: decision plane, control artifacts, fast-path substrate, static safety surfaces, trace intelligence, authority sandboxing, crash-only recovery, and transport experiments.
- Treat compatibility as a first-class artifact rather than an assumption hidden inside other validation beads.
- Build deterministic drills for incident handling, downgrade, rollback, and replay across track boundaries.
- Produce a matrix that distinguishes supported default combinations, supported-but-experimental combinations, and forbidden combinations.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic unit and E2E composition suites cover high-risk combinations across controllers, fast paths, static surfaces, trace intelligence, authority, recovery, and transport.
2. A compatibility matrix names supported, degraded, experimental, and forbidden combinations together with explicit fallback or downgrade behavior.
3. Structured logs, incident packets, and exact rerun commands let an operator reproduce any failing composition scenario directly.
4. Any cross-track regression in invariants, tail budgets, recovery behavior, authority containment, or diagnosability blocks closure.","notes":"Why this bead exists:
- Per-track validation is necessary but not sufficient. Users experience the composed system, not isolated tracks.
- The biggest late-stage risk in an ambitious runtime program is interaction failure: features that are individually correct but jointly unstable, opaque, or operationally painful.
- This bead is the plan-space answer to that failure mode.

Fallback doctrine:
- If a combination is not proven, it is explicitly marked unsupported or experimental.
- Default-ready closure requires both per-track green status and composition-level green status.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:10.178075830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T22:09:06.987652635Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T22:09:06.987628190Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","ci","release","runtime-ascension","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:10.178075830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:15.179026009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:17.692032263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:20.216484275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:22.736266190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:25.237995174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:27.734485164Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:55.194257877Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:30.257797392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:32.763947553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:35.339999483Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.8","title":"[AA-10][CI] Clear current repo-wide clippy frontier in sync/notify.rs","description":"Bounded support slice for AA-10 verification. The current repo-wide `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` frontier hit `src/sync/notify.rs` on an unused `current_generation` binding. Scope: confirm the working tree carries the minimal lint-safe fix, validate that the frontier is cleared with rch-offloaded clippy on the library target, and document the remaining all-targets verification blocker without broadening ownership beyond `src/sync/notify.rs`.","notes":"Completed against an already-present working-tree fix; broader all-targets clippy remains infra-blocked by rch timeout/local-fallback behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperGlacier","created_at":"2026-03-07T07:13:40Z","created_by":"CopperGlacier","updated_at":"2026-03-07T07:13:40Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T07:13:40Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","clippy","quality-gates","sync"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.8","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T07:13:40Z","created_by":"CopperGlacier","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2423,"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.8","author":"CopperGlacier","text":"Validated the current `src/sync/notify.rs` state as the lint-safe `_current_generation` form and confirmed the previous frontier is gone via `rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-copper-notify-lib-clippy cargo clippy -p asupersync --lib -- -D warnings` (pass). A broader `rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-copper-clippy-frontier-2 cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` timed out on the remote worker and attempted local fallback, which I aborted to honor the repo rule that cargo-heavy work must stay offloaded.","created_at":"2026-03-07T07:13:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.9","title":"[AA-10][CI] Clear current repo-wide clippy frontier in cx/scope.rs + sync/mutex.rs","description":"Bounded CI-quality slice under AA-10. Current remote lint frontier is in src/cx/scope.rs and src/sync/mutex.rs. Scope is to land minimal behavior-preserving fixes for the active library-target warnings, then re-run remote validation and report any remaining broader frontier without expanding ownership beyond those files.","notes":"Completed CI frontier slice. Landed: (1) behavior-preserving race_all control-flow cleanup in src/cx/scope.rs, (2) explicit pending-path guard release in src/sync/mutex.rs, (3) CatchUnwind/RegionRunner compile-frontier repair in src/cx/scope.rs with downstream constructor fixes in src/actor.rs and src/gen_server.rs. Remote validation: cargo check -p asupersync --lib exit=0, cargo clippy -p asupersync --lib -- -D warnings exit=0, cargo test -p asupersync --lib race_all_ exit=0, cargo test -p asupersync mutex_cancel exit=0. UBS diff scan completed; reported broad repo heuristics but formatting/clippy/check for scanned diff were clean. Remaining next repo-wide clippy frontier reported by peers is outside this bead: runtime guard-state warnings in src/runtime/blocking_pool.rs, src/runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs, src/runtime/scheduler/worker.rs, src/sync/pool.rs, src/util/arena.rs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanGrove","created_at":"2026-03-07T08:23:50.111159610Z","created_by":"CyanGrove","updated_at":"2026-03-07T08:55:54.302343535Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T08:55:54.302323267Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","clippy","cx","quality-gates","sync"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.9","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T08:23:50.111159610Z","created_by":"CyanGrove","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.2","title":"[WASM][Framework] Implement @asupersync/browser SDK surface","description":"Implement the higher-level @asupersync/browser SDK surface that sits above browser-core. This task should package the operational browser functionality into an opinionated but deterministic JS/TS API that ordinary browser consumers can use without manually managing every ABI detail.","notes":"Claimed after tracker unblock from asupersync-3qv04.3.5 closure. Browser SDK wrapper and contract coverage were already substantially present in-tree on the claimed file surface; this lane focused on validating and finalizing that surface. Validation evidence: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-cyangrove-352-clippy cargo clippy --test wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract -- -D warnings => exit=0; rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-cyangrove-352-check cargo check --test wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract => exit=0; rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-cyangrove-352-test-rerun cargo test --test wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract -- --nocapture => 21 passed. An earlier remote test attempt on worker vmi1149989 failed with no-space-left-on-device in /tmp before test execution; rerun on healthy worker vmi1152480 passed cleanly. No local cargo-heavy fallback accepted.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanGrove","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:17:33.519446608Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-07T09:25:41.010469659Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T09:25:41.010450443Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","sdk","typescript","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:05.365279607Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:05.403039851Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:05.445668528Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:05.487109059Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:17:33.519446608Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:05.325601911Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.3","title":"[WASM][Framework] Implement React provider/hook adapter with StrictMode correctness","description":"Implement the React-facing provider/hook layer, including StrictMode-safe lifecycle behavior, cleanup, and explicit cancellation ownership. This task should turn the current React semantic contracts into a real adapter package and prove that component remount/double-invocation does not leak work.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleFinch","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:17:33.560829120Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-07T15:51:56.105495829Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T15:51:56.105476262Z","close_reason":"Completed React provider/hook adapter + strict-mode lifecycle + contract evidence","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hooks","lifecycle","react","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:17:33.560829120Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:05.528995277Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.4","title":"[WASM][Framework] Implement Next client-hydrated bootstrap adapter","description":"Implement the Next client-hydrated bootstrap adapter so direct runtime execution works where the product actually intends it to work: hydrated client boundaries. This task owns hydration-safe init, route/cache invalidation handling, and the client-side ergonomics promised by the Next.js docs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OrangeOwl","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:17:33.601858055Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-07T15:51:32.084663501Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T15:51:32.084643944Z","close_reason":"Implemented the Next client-hydrated bootstrap adapter surface in packages/next/src/index.ts, added contract markers in tests/wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract.rs, and validated via rch cargo check/test + all-target cargo check/clippy. TypeScript workspace build remains upstream-blocked in packages/browser/browser-core and is not caused by this lane.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["client","hydration","next","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:17:33.601858055Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:05.569687828Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2428,"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.4","author":"OrangeOwl","text":"Implemented Next client bootstrap adapter in packages/next/src/index.ts and extended wasm_js_exports coverage markers. Validation: remote cargo check -q --test wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract passed; remote cargo test -q --test wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract passed (30 tests); remote cargo check --all-targets passed; remote cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed. Workspace TypeScript build is still blocked by pre-existing package resolution/type issues in packages/browser/browser-core; cargo fmt --check currently reports unrelated existing formatting drift outside this lane.","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:50:29Z"}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.5","title":"[WASM][Framework] Implement Next server/node bridge-only adapter","description":"Implement the serialized server/node bridge required by the product boundary. Server-side Next contexts are not intended to execute the runtime directly; they need a deliberate bridge surface that preserves outcome/cancellation semantics and avoids accidental ambient authority.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OrangeOwl","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:17:33.643032600Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-07T15:57:52.188377738Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bridge","next","server","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:17:33.643032600Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:05.610731811Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2430,"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.5","author":"OrangeOwl","text":"Initial implementation pass landed in packages/next/src/index.ts: added explicit server/node bridge-only adapter types + diagnostics/log helpers (, , , , request/response shapes, unsupported-runtime bridge error attachment). Extended tests/wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract.rs with server-bridge surface/policy markers. Validation so far: rch exec -- cargo test -q --test wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract ✅ (32 passed). Remaining work on this bead: decide whether the bridge surface needs more behavior helpers beyond the current serialized request/response + diagnostics-safe adapter.","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:57:42Z"},{"id":2431,"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.5","author":"OrangeOwl","text":"Initial implementation pass landed in packages/next/src/index.ts: added explicit server/node bridge-only adapter types and diagnostics/log helpers (NextBoundaryMode, NextRuntimeFallback, NextServerBridgeDiagnostics, NextServerBridgeAdapter, request/response shapes, unsupported-runtime bridge error attachment). Extended tests/wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract.rs with server-bridge surface/policy markers. Validation so far: rch exec -- cargo test -q --test wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract passed (32 tests).  {"id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.7","title":"[WASM][Framework] Provide explicit unsupported-runtime guards and actionable diagnostics","description":"Make misuse failures intentional and helpful. Browser Edition is not meant to run as a direct runtime in every JS environment, so the package surfaces and framework adapters need explicit unsupported-runtime guards with actionable diagnostics instead of opaque import-time or bootstrap-time crashes.

This bead covers user-visible errors and guidance for accidental server-side imports, Node/edge misuse, missing browser capabilities, or incompatible package entrypoints. It exists to make the system kinder and faster to debug for real consumers.","notes":"Implemented explicit unsupported-runtime diagnostics across the browser/react/next package surfaces.

Delivered:
- packages/browser/src/index.ts: Browser runtime capability snapshot, support diagnostics, actionable unsupported-runtime error construction/assertion, and guards in createBrowserRuntime/createBrowserScope
- packages/react/src/index.ts: React-specific runtime support diagnostics/assertion path with package-specific error identity
- packages/next/src/index.ts: Next client/server/edge runtime target diagnostics with explicit non-client guidance
- tests/wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract.rs: contract markers for browser/react/next unsupported-runtime helpers

Validation (all via rch):
- cargo test --test wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract -- --nocapture ✅ (24 passed)
- cargo check --test wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract ✅
- cargo clippy --test wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract -- -D warnings ✅
- cargo fmt --check -- tests/wasm_js_exports_coverage_contract.rs ✅","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanGrove","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-06T15:14:01.493441539Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-07T09:36:48.511513431Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T09:36:48.511463828Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","framework","ux","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:20:19.040950815Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:14:01.493441539Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:20:04.899138632Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-3qv04.6","title":"[WASM] Real consumer validation and compatibility matrix","description":"Replace documentation-only confidence with real consumer validation.

Current problem:
- many current wasm tests validate docs, policy files, workflow tokens, and matrix coverage;
- that is useful governance, but it does not prove that a user can install packages, build a real app, and get correct runtime behavior.

This track owns real consumer app builds, package install smoke, ABI compatibility matrix checks, and end-to-end browser behavior against packaged artifacts.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":1200,"created_at":"2026-03-06T07:15:08.120348439Z","created_by":"CopperForest","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:16:10.009772120Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bundlers","consumer","e2e","validation","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:15:08.120348439Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:03.809587771Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:19:03.764967765Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3qv04.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:16:10.009716817Z","created_by":"CopperForest","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2399,"issue_id":"asupersync-3qv04.6","author":"CopperForest","text":"[2026-03-06 CopperForest] A large portion of current WASM confidence comes from docs/policy/workflow-token validation.
